bottle — pin, name, and re-enter agent contexts

A bottle is an immutable snapshot of an agent conversation at a chosen
point: a frozen transcript plus its provenance. The workflow is three verbs:

  create    freeze the current (or a named) session into a new bottle
  decant    materialize a fresh, resumable session seeded from a bottle
  rebottle  freeze a decanted session back into a bottle, bumping its version

Names carry versions: `auth-expert` is the latest, `auth-expert@2` a fixed
snapshot. Rebottling records lineage (the parent bottle and the decant
session between them), so `log` shows the full provenance chain. Decanting
never mutates a bottle — each decant spawns a fresh conversation and runs
against the repo as it is today (branch and sha are recorded, not restored).

Self-bottling trims the in-flight turn: a bottle of your own live session is
cut at the last completed turn, dropping the running `bottle create` call.

v1 is Claude-only. Any agent can inspect bottles and decant into a pane, but
self-bottling needs a Claude session id ($CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID); from
another harness pass `--session ID` or `--last`.

Commands:
  create     Snapshot a session into a new bottle
  decant     Materialize a fresh session from a bottle and resume it
  rebottle   Re-bottle a decanted session, bumping the version
  list       Table of bottles: name, latest version, count, age, note
  log        Version chain with provenance
  show       Full metadata plus a transcript preview
  rename     Move all versions to a new name
  note       Set or replace the free-text note
  prune      Drop decant entries whose session files are gone
  rm         Delete one version, or the whole name
  artifacts  List or extract attached artifacts
  sync       Converge this store with its git remote

Run `bottle <command> --help` for usage, examples, and pitfalls.
